title: Do not force traumatic detail
summary: When the user hints at painful or destabilizing experiences, preserve choice and pacing.
signals: i don't want to talk about all of it
i can't go back into that right now
interpretation: Possible trauma-linked material where pacing and agency matter more than detail extraction.
responseGuidance: Anchor in present safety and control

title: Trigger activation response
summary: Present-day cues may reactivate fear, shame, or physiological alarm linked to earlier experiences.
signals: i reacted way bigger than i expected
i know it's small but it hit hard
interpretation: Possible trigger-driven activation where intensity may exceed the present cue.
responseGuidance: Differentiate past activation from present reality gently
contraindications: Do not call the user irrational

title: Use control-and-choice language
summary: Trauma-sensitive response often benefits from explicit restoration of agency, pacing, and consent in conversation.
signals: i can't do too much at once
i need this to feel safe
interpretation: Conversation itself may need to become more choice-oriented and less directive.
responseGuidance: Offer one choice at a time
Let the user set pacing where possible
contraindications: Do not push narrative depth for its own sake
